prompt:
A researcher claims that recycling rate in the town climbed steadily from 2019 to 2022.

Which choice best uses data from the table to support the researcher's claim?

choices.rationale: The table shows the value changing every year, not holding steady.

choices.text: The value peaked in 2021 at 40 percent and then reversed course for the rest of the period.
choices.rationale: The extreme value is at an endpoint, not the second year shown.

choices.text: Each year the value went up, climbing from 36 to 48 percent across the period.
choices.rationale: Correct. This matches the steady, one-direction trend the claim describes across all the years shown.

choices.text: The value drifted down in some years and up in others rather than moving in one direction.
choices.rationale: Every year moves the same direction, so this misreads the table.

explanation: The supporting statement must reflect the table's actual, consistent trend across all the years shown.

table.caption: Recycling rate in the town (percent)
